📏 Rule of Three Calculator

Solve direct and inverse proportions instantly by entering just three known values.

?
Enter the three known values

How to use the rule of three

The rule of three is a method to solve proportions when three of the four values are known. It is one of the most practical mathematical tools in everyday life, used for calculating prices by quantity, scaling recipes, converting units, calculating work times, reading map scales and much more.

Direct proportion example: If 3 kg of apples cost £7, how much do 5 kg cost? Answer: (7 × 5) ÷ 3 = £11.67
Inverse proportion example: If 4 workers finish a job in 6 days, how long would 1 worker take? Answer: (4 × 6) ÷ 1 = 24 days

What is the rule of three?

The rule of three is a mathematical method to find an unknown value when three related values are known. It comes in two forms — direct proportion (both values increase or decrease together) and inverse proportion (one increases as the other decreases).

Frequently asked questions

When is proportion direct vs inverse?

Direct: more kilometres → more fuel. Inverse: more workers → fewer days to finish. If the two quantities move in the same direction it is direct; opposite directions means inverse.

What is cross multiplication?

Cross multiplication is the technique used to solve proportions — multiply the diagonal values and divide. For direct proportion: X = (B × C) ÷ A.

Can I solve "if 3 kg cost £7, how much do 5 kg cost"?

Yes. Enter A=3, B=7, C=5 with direct proportion. The result X = (7×5)÷3 = £11.67.
